title: corymbia hylandii
intro: Corymbia hylandii, also known as the Bell-Figure Gum, is a species of tree native to Australia. It has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Corymbia hylandii are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and bark are used to prepare teas, infusions, and poultices for various health ailments.
